text: In software development, testing plays a crucial role in ensuring that code works correctly. Unit tests check individual components, like functions or methods, to verify they behave as expected. Integration tests examine how different parts of the system work together. With test-driven development, developers write a failing test first, then write code to pass it, and finally refactor. This approach helps catch bugs early and creates a clear design. Overall, thorough testing gives developers confidence to make changes or add features without fear of breaking existing functionality. It saves time and improves the quality of the final product.
